start.speaker: Obstinate
start.text: So you would leave your house, your trade, and your name for a rumor of a Celestial City? Come back before shame becomes the only thing you own.

choices.text: I must go forward. Staying would only make my fear look respectable.
You are blind if you cannot see the fire.
Maybe you are right. Maybe I am only frightened.

tempt.speaker: Obstinate
tempt.text: At last, sense. Put down the book, lose the burden, and become ordinary again.

choices.text: No. The burden remains, and the light is still there. I am going.

leaves.speaker: Obstinate
leaves.text: Then go your way. I will not chase a man determined to be ruined by hope.

leaves_angry.speaker: Obstinate
leaves_angry.text: Proud words from a burdened man. Go, then, and see how long your courage lasts.
